security_gap.gap_description: Airdrop eligibility proof may be forged or misused, allowing unauthorized claims.

actor_risk.description: An external actor attempts to claim BERA tokens they are not entitled to.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful spoofing leads to unauthorized acquisition of the native asset ($BERA).

assumptions: The mechanism for verifying airdrop eligibility (e.g., NFT ownership, social link proof) has a vulnerability.
An attacker can obtain, forge, or reuse eligibility proofs belonging to others.

mitigation.strategy: Strengthen eligibility verification logic; implement unique, non-reusable claim proofs.

description: An attacker may impersonate an eligible user or exploit a vulnerability in the claim process to claim BERA airdrop tokens allocated to others.

security_gap.gap_description: DEX smart contract may not sufficiently validate swap parameters (e.g., slippage) or protect against flash loan attacks.

actor_risk.description: An external actor attempts to manipulate swap execution or outcomes.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful tampering can lead to unfair swap prices, asset loss for users, or liquidity pool drain.

assumptions: The BEX smart contract has a vulnerability related to swap execution, pricing, or parameter validation.
An attacker can craft a transaction to exploit this vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Implement stringent slippage checks, utilize robust price oracles, and apply reentrancy guards.

description: An attacker may exploit vulnerabilities in the BEX smart contract to tamper with swap parameters or execution, leading to manipulated prices or unauthorized access to liquidity.

security_gap.gap_description: High transaction volume or large swaps can exhaust liquidity or congest the BEX contract, preventing legitimate users from trading.

actor_risk.description: An external actor attempts to disrupt BEX operations through excessive transactions or liquidity manipulation.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful DoS impairs the core functionality of the BEX for all users.

assumptions: The BEX smart contract or underlying blockchain infrastructure is susceptible to resource exhaustion from high transaction volume or specific swap patterns.
An attacker has sufficient resources (e.g., capital for large swaps, BERA for gas) to execute a DoS attack.

mitigation.strategy: Implement transaction rate limits or dynamic fees; ensure sufficient liquidity and network capacity.

description: An attacker could flood the BEX with transactions or execute large, strategic swaps to deplete liquidity pools or congest the network, causing a denial of service for other users.

security_gap.gap_description: HoneySwap vault or factory smart contract may allow manipulation of minting/redemption amounts or bypass collateral requirements.

actor_risk.description: An external actor attempts to mint HONEY without sufficient collateral or manipulate redemption amounts.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful tampering leads to unauthorized HONEY creation or theft of deposited collateral.

assumptions: The HoneySwap vault or factory smart contract contains a vulnerability in its minting or redemption logic.
An attacker can craft a transaction to exploit this vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Rigorously validate mint/redeem parameters against collateral amounts and rates; perform extensive smart contract audits.

description: An attacker could exploit vulnerabilities in the HoneySwap smart contracts to tamper with the minting or redemption process, potentially minting unauthorized HONEY or stealing collateral.

security_gap.gap_description: BEX smart contract may allow manipulating liquidity provision parameters or calculations, leading to incorrect LP token issuance or asset distribution.

actor_risk.description: An external actor attempts to manipulate the liquidity provision process for personal gain.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful tampering could result in unfair distribution of LP tokens or affect pool integrity.

assumptions: The BEX smart contract has a vulnerability in the logic for adding liquidity and calculating LP tokens.
An attacker can submit crafted parameters to exploit this vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust validation and auditing for liquidity addition calculations and LP token issuance.

description: An attacker could tamper with the liquidity provision process on BEX by exploiting flaws in the smart contract logic, potentially receiving an unfair amount of LP tokens or disrupting the pool's balance.

security_gap.gap_description: BEX smart contract may not sufficiently verify ownership or validity of LP tokens during withdrawal.

actor_risk.description: An external actor attempts to withdraw liquidity using LP tokens they do not legitimately own.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful spoofing allows unauthorized removal of assets from liquidity pools.

assumptions: The BEX smart contract has a vulnerability allowing withdrawal with invalid or stolen LP token proofs.
An attacker can acquire or forge proofs of LP token ownership for tokens they don't own.

mitigation.strategy: Enforce strict verification of LP token ownership via standard ERC-20 mechanisms within the withdrawal logic.

description: An attacker could potentially impersonate a legitimate liquidity provider by spoofing LP token ownership to withdraw assets from BEX liquidity pools they are not entitled to.

security_gap.gap_description: BEX smart contract may allow manipulation of withdrawal calculations or asset distribution based on LP tokens.

actor_risk.description: An external actor attempts to manipulate liquidity withdrawal amounts or asset distribution.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Successful tampering can lead to over-withdrawal of assets or unfair distribution from pools.

assumptions: The BEX smart contract has a vulnerability in the logic for withdrawing liquidity and calculating asset returns based on LP tokens.
An attacker can submit crafted parameters to exploit this vulnerability.

mitigation.strategy: Rigorously validate withdrawal amounts against LP token balance and apply accurate calculations for asset returns.

description: An attacker could tamper with the liquidity withdrawal process on BEX by exploiting flaws in the smart contract logic, potentially withdrawing more assets than their LP tokens entitle them to.
